question: Can I file an appeal if my application for citizenship is rejected?
answer: Yes You can file an appeal when the Prosecutor of the King gives a negative opinion on your application for citizenship. If the four-month deadline in which the Prosecutor of the King must respond has not yet expired, you can send him a letter explaining your situation in detail; asking him to change his mind. If the four-month deadline in which the Prosecutor of the King must respond has expired: you can send a registered letter to the civil registry officer of your municipality. You have 15 days to do so from the receipt of the decision. You can also file an appeal with the Court of Appeal.

question: How can I apply for my survivor's pension?
answer: If your spouse was already retired, you should not apply for your survivor's pension. If your spouse was not yet retired, you should apply for your survivor's pension: on the Social Security website; or to your municipality; or to a Pointpension. Do not delay in submitting your application. If you submit it less than 12 months after the death of your spouse, you will receive the first payment: on the 1st day of the month following his death if he was an employee.

question: I want to get a divorce because of irreparable disunity.
answer: Yes, it is the judge who sets this limit in the divorce judgment. In principle, the duration of alimony after divorce is limited to a maximum of the duration of the marriage. But the judge can set it for a shorter duration. He always decides depending on the concrete situation of the ex-spouses. At the end of this period, the judge can extend the payment of alimony. The person who receives alimony must prove that due to exceptional circumstances and for reasons beyond his control, he is still in a state of need.

question: What is the right to be forgotten in insurance matters?
answer: The right to be forgotten exists for: outstanding balance insurance (mortgage credit and professional credit) since 1 February 2020 for new contracts; guaranteed income insurance since 1 February 2022 for new contracts. It is the right for a person cured of cancer or a chronic disease to be able to take out insurance. To be considered cured, the person must wait a certain period of time. The general deadline is 8 years (from 1 January 2025 the general deadline will be 5 years).

question: I'm starting a new family, what's going on with family allowances in Wallonia?
answer: You can group your children and your partner's children for family allowances. To do this, you must: be domiciled at the same address as your partner; or prove by other means that you live together. You can also group children raised by another person with whom you live: your spouse; your relative or ally up to the 3rd degree; a person with whom you declare to form a de facto household. For more information, see the Famiwal or Aviq (Agency for a Quality Life) website.

question: My legal cohabitant has died. What are my rights in his estate?
answer: Attention, this sheet only applies to people who have made a declaration of legal cohabitation to the municipality. To learn more about legal cohabitation, go to the "situation of couples" section of the Family section of this database. At the death of your legal cohabitant, you inherit the usufruct on the family housing and the furniture that is there. After his death, you can continue to live in the legal housing, even if it does not belong to you. If your cohabitant was a tenant, you inherit the lease.

question: Who has to pay for the repair of the damage caused during a burglary in my home in Wallonia?
answer: The distinction between these two terms is not always very clear. The rules outlined below are imperative. As a tenant, you must repair all damage to the rented dwelling. Unless you can prove that it is not your fault. You must prove that it is due to old age, normal wear and tear or force majeure. A burglary or attempted burglary is a case of force majeure.

question: How do I send my notice to the landlord in Wallonia?
answer: You must give your notice either: by registered mail; via a bailiff; directly into the hands of the landlord. He must sign the double and give it to you. The date of receipt must be indicated. But you can always agree with the landlord on other ways to send the notice. It is preferable to provide for these other ways in the lease contract. Be careful, if you have signed the lease with other people, all these people must sign the notice for it to be valid.

question: I'm a parent. Can I index the food contribution? How do I calculate this indexation?
answer: Yes, the law provides that the food contribution set in an agreement or in a judgment is always indexed, even if this has not been foreseen. It is not you who decides the amount and time of the indexation. The basic food contribution is adapted according to the consumer price index of the month preceding the month in which the judgment was pronounced (or the adopted agreement). For example: A judgment issued in July 2018 sets a food contribution of EUR 125 in July each year, the amount can be indexed.
topics: Family, alimony obligations, alimony contributions (for children)

question: Am I still entitled to family allowances after my studies in Brussels?
answer: Yes, under certain conditions. You are under 25 years old. You must be registered as a jobseeker, at Actiris. You are in a job placement course for one year from your registration as a jobseeker. You do not work more than 240 hours per quarter. You are no longer subject to compulsory education. You must be involuntarily unemployed. You cannot refuse a suitable job, and you must look for a job (a rigorous control process by Actiris, with regular assessments, etc.). For more information, see the section "Unemployment after studying". You keep your family allowance for 1 year after studying, unless you are too late.

question: What can the child do in case of conflict with his guardian?
answer: In the event of a serious conflict between the guardian and the minor child, or between the surrogate guardian and the minor child, the child may notify the King's Prosecutor. He may contact him orally or by mail. But the child must be at least 12 years old to contact the King's Prosecutor: for problems concerning his person, the child must be at least 12 years old; for problems concerning his property, the child must be at least 15 years old.

question: My landlord is not responding, can I terminate the lease in Wallonia?
answer: You can always terminate the lease amicably if your landlord agrees. If no amicable agreement can be found, you will have to file an action with the justice of the peace. You must ask for the lease to be terminated because of the landlord's fault. The judge is not obliged to agree with you. He will hear everyone's arguments and decide the most appropriate penalty (break of lease, conviction to do the work, etc.).

question: I am self-employed, am I entitled to social integration income (RIS)?
answer: Yes. If you meet the other conditions, you are entitled to RIS: even if you do not have a home (i.e. an official registration in the population register); and even if you do not have housing. You must provide proof that you are "usually and permanently" in Belgium. You must address your request for assistance to the CPAS at your place of de facto residence. This is not always easy to determine, since the homeless sometimes move a lot. In practice, in general, the CPAS registers you at the address of the CPAS, as a "reference address".
topics: Social protection, CPAS aid, social integration income (RIS), having his effective residence in Belgium

question: My landlord terminated the nine-year lease. Can I leave before the end of the notice period in Wallonia?
answer: Yes. If you are a tenant: of a nine-year main residence lease, and you receive six months' notice from your landlord, you can terminate the lease in turn by registered mail with a one-month notice period. This is called "counter-notice". The one-month period begins on the first day of the month following the dispatch of your "counter-notice". If the notice is given, the counter-notice begins on 01.01.2019 and ends on 28.02.2019.

question: Tax or fee, what difference does it make?
answer: If the municipality opts for the fee system, it is civil law and more specifically the common law of obligations that will apply between the municipality and you. The municipality's fee regulation must be regarded as a set of general contractual conditions to which you have voluntarily acceded by parking your vehicle in an area where such a fee regulation is applicable. The fee regulation is a kind of concession that the municipality accepts by deciding to use the service offered by the municipality.

question: Can my landlord increase the rent during the lease in Brussels?
answer: Your landlord may increase the rent if: he has actually done the energy performance work or the adaptation of the housing to your disability; he has increased the rent; he has obtained your disability on an increase in the rent. The landlord must pay the rent at least 1% before the work is completed. Your landlord has never been obliged to mark your agreement on an increase in the rent. At the end of the year, your landlord may request an increase in the rent.

question: Can I terminate my short-term lease before it expires in Wallonia?
answer: Yes. You can terminate the lease at any time. You must: send your notice 3 months in advance, and pay compensation equivalent to 1 month's rent. The notice begins to run on the 1st day of the month following that during which the landlord receives it. For more information on the notice, see the sheet "How to send my notice to the landlord?" You can also always put it there: by mutual agreement with your landlord: for more information on this, see the question "How to terminate the lease by mutual agreement?"

question: I'm leaving a shelter, which CPAS should I go to for a rental guarantee?
answer: You must contact the CPAS of the municipality in which your dwelling is located, which can help you to establish a rental guarantee.

question: I signed a declaration of legal cohabitation. The landlord sent his notice to only one legal cohabitant, is that valid?
answer: No. The notice sent to a single legal cohabitant is not valid. As long as the legal cohabitation lasts, the couple benefits from the protection of the family housing, i.e. the protection of the property that serves as housing for the family. The protection of the housing lasts even if the couple is de facto separated. The landlord must address his notice to each legal cohabitant. This principle applies even if the lease was signed by only one person, and even if the declaration of legal cohabitation took place during the lease. The landlord must have been warned of the legal cohabitation of his tenant.
topics: Family, situation of couples, legal cohabitation

question: What should I do if I do not receive a decision regarding the RIS?
answer: If you do not receive a decision within 30 days of the introduction of your application: 1. Contact your social worker: ask him why you have not yet received a decision. Perhaps the CPAS is still waiting for a document or evidence from you. 2. Contact a social worker, a lawyer or a lawyer. This person can: intervene with the CPAS to try to unblock your case; file an appeal before the court against this lack of decision.

question: My landlord is not responding, can I stop paying my rent or reduce it in Wallonia?
answer: In principle, you can't stop paying your rent or even reduce it, even if your landlord doesn't do the necessary work. Paying the rent is the main obligation of the tenant. If you don't pay your rent, you're in the wrong. Your landlord can ask the justice of the peace to break the lease at your expense.

question: I want to get a divorce because of irreparable separation. Can I ask my husband or wife for alimony?
answer: Yes. The family judge can grant a spouse alimony after divorce if he is in a state of need. The spouse who is in need must expressly ask the judge during the divorce proceedings to have alimony. It is never granted ex officio by the judge. The judge can refuse this request for alimony after divorce if the other spouse proves: that the spouse who asks for this alimony has committed a serious offense and; that this offense has made common life impossible.

question: I have completed an alternance training, will the duration of my internship be reduced?
answer: Before going any further, this sheet contains only general information. For more information, please contact: the National Employment Office (ONEM); your paying agency (union or CAPAC). Yes. After your studies, you are not entitled to unemployment benefits immediately. You must first undergo a vocational integration internship (before the waiting internship). question: Within what time do I have to file my appeal if the CPAS does not take a decision?
answer: You must file your appeal within 3 months of the CPAS's "confirmation of the absence of a decision". But the law does not specify when there is a "confirmation of the absence of a decision". Generally, the 3 months are calculated from the end of the deadline: 1 month if you have filed a request for social assistance; 30 days if you have filed a request for social integration income (CPRIS). You can also send a letter to the CPAS, stating that no decision has been made within the 3 month deadline.

question: Does the nine-year lease end automatically when it expires in Wallonia?
answer: No, the lease does not end automatically at the scheduled deadline. In order for the 9-year lease to end, one of the parties (you or the landlord) must send a notice: the landlord must send his notice at least 6 months before the end of the lease. The tenant must send his notice at least 3 months before the end of the lease. For more information, see our sheet "How to send my notice to the landlord?". If neither of you sends a notice, the lease is extended by 3 years on the same terms (including rent).

question: My landlord has not registered the lease. Can I terminate the lease without notice or compensation in Brussels?
answer: You must leave your dwelling without notice or compensation, if the lease is not registered, but only under 2 conditions: 2 months must have elapsed since the signing of the lease (this is the period in which the landlord must have the lease registered); You must remind your landlord to register the lease within 1 month. If all these conditions are met, you must comply with the reminder. You must send your reminder orally. You will find a model of reminder in the standard documents. If your landlord registers the lease within 1 month, you cannot break the lease without notice or compensation.

question: Can my landlord terminate the long-term lease before it expires in Brussels?
answer: Before going any further, a long-term lease is a lease of more than 9 years. This sheet was updated more than a year ago. He can only terminate the lease under the following conditions: at the end of the lease, with six months' notice (the notice must be given six months before the expected expiration date); at the end of each three-year lease, but with six months' notice and an indemnity of nine months' rent if it's the end of the first three-year lease; six months' rent if it's the end of the second three-year lease; three months' rent if it's the end of the third lease; but always with his family.

question: Is the lease registration mandatory in Brussels?

answer: Yes. The amount of alimony after divorce can always be adjusted if new circumstances arise that are independent of your will. For example, loss of employment, major health problems are new circumstances that are independent of your will. However, check in your pre-divorce agreements whether this possibility has not been excluded. The agreements must provide for the circumstances and modalities for changing the amount of alimony. In case of conflict, you can contact the family judge.

question: I don ⁇ t agree with the doctor ⁇ s decision. Can I challenge it?
answer: Yes. To do this, you must file an appeal with the labour court within 3 months of the notification of the decision. For more information, see our section <unk> Contesting a social security decision <unk> During the procedure, you may be entitled to temporary unemployment benefits for medical force majeure, if your disability is temporary. For this, your employer must provide you with the C3.2A control form every month. You must complete this form and submit it at the end of the month to your paying body (CAPAC or union).

question: Does the long-term lease end automatically when it expires in Brussels?
answer: Before we go any further, a long-term lease is a lease of more than 9 years. This sheet was updated more than a year ago. For the long-term lease to end, one of the parties (you or the landlord) must send a notice: the landlord must send his notice at least 6 months before the end of the lease. question: What if I don't agree with the decision of the CPAS in Wallonia?
answer: You can: ask the CPAS to review its decision; challenge the decision before the labour court. The appeal before the labour court must be lodged by an application within 3 months. It is free of charge. You will find information on this possibility of appeal and the procedure to be followed in the CPAS decision. question: I am a tenant, do I have to calculate the reference rent in Brussels?
answer: No. The landlord has to calculate the reference rent. He has to indicate it in the lease. Be careful, the reference rent is not the rent you pay. If your lease started before December 2, 2021, the landlord has to make an amendment to the lease. In this amendment, he specifies the reference rent. To know the reference rent, the landlord has to consult the indicative reference rent grid in Brussels.

answer: Yes, you can ask to modify your individualized social integration project (PIIS). The PIIS can be modified at the request of each of the parties during its execution. But in practice, there are often few possibilities to obtain a modification of the "proposed" contract. There is often no real negotiation possible. answer: No. Your landlord can't index the rent if at the time of his request, there is no PEB certificate for your housing. question: I receive benefits from the mutual: can I be a close helper?

question: What can I do in case of a problem of competence of the CPAS?

question: Is my lease really a short-term lease in Wallonia?
answer: A short-term lease is a lease signed for 3 years or less. For a lease concluded before September 2018: The short-term lease can only be renewed once under the same conditions (including rent). This is called an extension. This must be done in writing (this can be a clause in the original lease). The total duration cannot exceed 3 years.

answer: You have the right to occupy your apartment as you wish, but you must respect your obligations and the rights of others, such as your roommates, and not interfere with them. If the lease agreement limits the accommodation of friends, the lease agreement may provide for restrictions on the accommodation of friends. For example, when the landlord lives in the same building as the free tenant, these restrictions may not be excessive.

In this case, you must sign the duplicate and hand it over to the landlord. The date of receipt must be indicated. question: What do I risk if I don ⁇ t respect my PIIS and I get a RIS?
answer: If you do not comply with your Individualized Social Integration Project (IPIS) without a legitimate reason, you may be suspended by the CPAS for a maximum of 1 month. The duration of the suspension can be up to 3 months if you do not comply with your IPIS within 1 year. The suspension begins: at the earliest the day after the notification of the CPAS' decision to suspend the payment of RISIS; at the latest on the 1st day of the 3rd month after the decision to suspend the payment of RISIS.

question: Is repainting a repair made necessary by obsolescence, normal wear and tear or force majeure in Wallonia?
answer: Before we go any further, we are talking about maintenance work (keeping the thing in good condition) and repair work (repairing the damaged part). The distinction between these two terms is not always very clear. The following rules are imperative. Old age and normal wear and tear depend on the materials and parts of the property (repairing the floor, walls, plumbing, chassis, boiler, etc.). The passage of time does not produce the same damage; therefore, the same damage can be caused by the type of materials or the part of land concerned. For example, paint is removed after 15 years of occupancy.

question: Who should I contact if I have a problem with the ONEM?
answer: You have several possibilities. You can challenge the decision before the labour court. Look in the decision taken by the ONEM, you will find information: To whom to address your appeal? How? Within what time? Your application must be submitted by request. Application templates are available at the registry of the labour court. question: I signed a declaration of legal cohabitation. Can I kick my legal cohabitant out?
answer: No. As legal cohabitants, your family housing is protected. The purpose of family housing protection is to ensure security and stability for family members. A legal cohabitant cannot deprive the other of the opportunity to occupy the family housing. For example, a legal cohabitant cannot: terminate the lease alone; kick the other out; sell the housing without the other's consent, even if he is the sole owner. The furniture of the housing is also protected.
topics: Family, situation of couples, legal cohabitation

question: Can I declare in advance that I refuse certain treatment (therapeutic stubbornness)?
answer: Yes, you can declare in advance that you refuse certain treatments for the day you are unable to do so. For example: I refuse to be resuscitated; I refuse to be fed personally; if I can no longer feed myself by my own means. Your declaration must refer to one or more specific interventions.

question: I want to study in Belgium. What conditions must my guarantor meet?
answer: Before going any further, this question only applies to non-European students. One of the conditions for coming to study in Belgium is to have sufficient resources. You can prove this condition by having your guarantor fill out a commitment form (annex 32). But not everyone can be a guarantor. There are several conditions to be met. Your guarantor must be: a natural person (i.e. not a legal person, e.g. an association or a company, etc.); at least 18 years old or emancipated; Belgian; or European resident in Belgium or another country of the European Union; or a foreigner authorized to stay indefinitely.

question: Can I study if I get the RIS?

However, the CPAS can exempt you from proving your willingness to work, if this is justified on grounds of equity. One of the exceptions that allows you to be exempt is the fact of starting, resuming or continuing your studies.

question: Who organizes and pays for funerals when there is no heir?
answer: If the deceased was not registered anywhere, it is the municipality of the place of death that has to take care of the funeral. question: How to file an appeal against the decision of the CPAS before the Labour Court?
answer: Your written appeal may be a simple letter to the Labour Court. This letter should at least mention: your name, address and national registration number; the CPAS you are challenging the decision; the date; your signature. Most registries have pre-printed documents that you just have to complete. It is often useful to attach a copy of the decision (or decisions) you are challenging. Your appeal does not necessarily have to be reasoned. Sometimes it is better not to develop the arguments until we have been able to do a thorough analysis of the case and take note of the administrative problem of the CPAS (which will be part of the procedure).

question: Can I go abroad and keep my allowance for elderly care in Wallonia?
answer: Yes. You can go abroad and continue to receive your elderly care allowance (APA). If you stay abroad for more than 90 consecutive days a year, you must: notify your mutual APA service at least 1 month before your departure; indicate the estimated duration of your stay; indicate the reasons for your stay; send a life certificate to your mutual APA service every year. If you do not send your life certificate, your mutual APA service will send you a life certificate. You have 30 days after receiving the reminder to send your life certificate. If you do not send it within this period, payment is suspended.

question: What is an Individualized Social Integration Project (PIIS) (social aid)?
answer: The individual social integration project (PIIS) is a contract that the CPAS concludes with the aid applicant. Specifically, it is a contract that the aid applicant and the CPAS sign. The aid applicant undertakes to make certain efforts or steps to improve his situation, such as following training, finding housing, repaying his debts, etc. The CPAS undertakes to help him and to accompany him with certain measures. The CPAS must know the aspirations, skills, qualifications and needs of the aid applicant and the possibilities of the CPAS.

question: What if the out-of-court warrant goes wrong?
answer: You, the trustee or any person who has an interest can at any time ask the justice of the peace to verify your situation. The judge checks whether the trustee is correctly executing the mandate, and whether the actions he is taking are in your best interest. If the justice of the peace finds that the mandate is not the best protection in your situation, he can turn the mandate into a measure of administration of property and/or the person.

question: What's the reference address for?

question: Do I have to give full notice if I get fired?

question: Can I end the lifetime lease in Brussels?
answer: You must pay an indemnity to your landlord if you terminate the lease for the first three years. The indemnity is determined by the date of entry into the premises. It is equivalent to: 3 months of rent if you leave during the first year; 2 months of rent if you leave during the second year; 1 month of rent if you leave during the third year; no indemnity if you leave after the first three years.

question: Can I terminate the nine-year lease before it expires in Wallonia?

question: Can the protected person make a will or a donation?
answer: This depends on the decision of the justice of the peace. The justice of the peace must follow a list of acts, and decide for each one whether the protected person is capable or incapable. The judge can add other acts to his decision, but he must at least decide for the acts on this list. The will and the donation are part of this list. If the person is declared incapable of making a donation or writing a will, the administrator of property or the person's estate cannot represent him and make it in his name.

question: Can I file an appeal if the CPAS does not make a decision?
answer: Yes. If it does not make a decision within this period, you can appeal to the labour court against this lack of decision. The court then notes the failure of the CPAS and makes a decision in its place... but this will take some time.

question: Do I have the right to pass on empty ground?
answer: Yes, if you comply with all these conditions. Everyone has the right to pass on vacant land if: the owner has not made it clear that access is prohibited; and the land is neither built, nor cultivated, nor fenced. This right is temporary. For example: passing, strolling, picnicking, playing for a few hours, etc. This right cannot harm the owner of the land or cause him damage.

question: I do not agree with the decision of the Directorate-General for Disabled Persons regarding my integration allowance (AI). What should I do?

question: Which establishments are affected by this exception to the competence of the CPAS?

question: Can I terminate the nine-year lease before it expires in Brussels?
